idea.title: Word Sweep
idea.hook: Drag across a grimy grid of letters to spell words — each word washes its letters clean and fresh tiles tumble in.
idea.hook_ru: Веди пальцем по грязной сетке букв и составляй слова — каждое слово отмывает свои буквы дочиста, а сверху падают свежие плитки.
idea.prototype: Boggle/Wordscapes-style drag-to-spell on an adjacency grid (8-directional incl. diagonals, min 3 letters), valid words wash clean with sparkles and cascade-refill from the top; exponential length scoring + clean-cascade chains + golden x2 bonus tiles; level-based progression (8 levels, grid grows, grime speeds up).
idea.mutation_of: Boggle/Wordscapes drag-to-spell × satisfying clean-cascade (match-3 style gravity refill) × Tetris-style rising-garbage pressure
idea.why_novel: Word games on our portfolio are 100% liked and the cleaning/clearing crossover is our strongest family at 93% liked; this sits in BOTH. The genuinely new beat is using RISING GRIME ROWS as the lose pressure for a word game (spatial, not a timer) where long words physically scrub grime away — fusing the cozy word-search loop with Tetris-like encroachment so the player must hunt longer words under spatial pressure. Localises cleanly (EN + RU dictionaries shipped).
idea.non_timer_pressure: rising grime rows (locked grimy tiles push up from the bottom every few seconds, shoving the board upward; words of 5+ letters scrub a grime row back down)
idea.lose_condition: grimeRows >= GRID_H — grime fills the board to the top row (gs='GAMEOVER'); reachable within ~60s of idle/random play

sources.title: Top 50 UK Game Makers 2026 / global-first design

synthesizer_brief: Cross today's two strongest portfolio signals — word games (100% liked) and the cleaning/clearing crossover (93% liked) — into one mechanic that lives in both. A drag-to-spell word grid where spelling a word literally washes its grimy letters clean (the cleanup payoff) and the pressure is a rising wall of grime (spatial, non-timer) that only long words can shove back. Picked specifically because it localises cleanly: ships compact EN + RU dictionaries and full EN+RU HUD/tutorial via GF.t(), aligning with the tier-1 localization + global-first-design signals from the digest.

alternatives.title: Tower Balance
alternatives.hook: Stack mismatched blocks on a wobbling tower and keep its center of mass over the base as the wind picks up.
alternatives.why_novel: Replaces the usual timing-tap stacking with a continuous balance-pressure axis (COM vs base) — non-timer physical pressure. Deduped today (already on the digest list, overlaps wobble_stack).

alternatives.title: Donut Drift Arena
alternatives.hook: Drift a donut around a shrinking arena, smearing a glaze trail to claim territory before rivals box you in.
alternatives.mutation_of: Splatoon-style territory paint × drift driving × shrinking-arena pressure
alternatives.why_novel: Territory % + shrinking arena as the squeeze (spatial pressure). Deduped — overlaps existing donut_drift / pong_wars_reverse.

alternatives.title: Bubble Pop Lab
alternatives.hook: Mix colored reagents into bubbles and pop matching clusters before the foam overflows the beaker.
alternatives.mutation_of: Bubble-shooter × color-mix chemistry × rising-foam overflow pressure
alternatives.why_novel: Color-combine crafting on top of cluster-pop, with rising foam as the non-timer squeeze. Deduped — overlaps existing bubble_pop.

alternatives.title: Hue Sort Valves
alternatives.hook: Route colored liquid through a pipe junction into the right tanks before the overflow tank fills.
alternatives.why_novel: Sort puzzle where YOU control the flow routing in real time rather than picking from stacks; overflow tank is the spatial lose condition (non-timer). On-the-table color-sort alternate, not built today.

alternatives.title: Core Cracker
alternatives.hook: Tap-mine a giant rock core; each crack spawns helpers that auto-chip while you aim taps at the weak seams.
alternatives.mutation_of: Idle incremental clicker × rock-breaking × weak-point aiming
alternatives.why_novel: Aimed-tap skill layer on an idle rock-breaker — depth comes from targeting seams, not pure DPS. Pressure is a re-hardening core (it heals if you stop). On-the-table rock-breaking incremental alternate, not built today.
